name: Task.Parallel

unions.name: ListMsg
unions.comment: Opaque type for updating state of task lists.

unions.name: ListState
unions.comment: Opaque type for storing state of task lists.

unions.name: Msg2
unions.comment: Opaque type for updating state of tasks.

unions.name: State2
unions.comment: Opaque type for storing state of tasks.

values.name: attempt
values.comment:
Attempt a single task. The benefit of this over Task.attempt is that it
handles routing the result to the provided success and failure messages. You can
reuse those error messages for different tasks if they're handled the same way.
Since there are no other tasks, you don't need to manage any additional updates.

    type Msg
        = ErrorOcurred Http.Error
        | FetchCompleted MyData

    doTask : Cmd Msg
    doTask =
        attempt FetchCompleted ErrorOcurred fetchMyData
values.type: (a -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> Task.Task x a -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: attempt2
values.comment:
Attempt two tasks which will send an update when either all tasks finish
successfully or one fails. The returned `State` will be used in your main
update function to call [`update`](#update) and pass internal messages.

    type Msg
        = TaskStateUpdated (Task.Parallel.Msg2 Http.Error String Int)
        | OneTaskFailed Http.Error
        | AllTasksCompleted String Int

    doTask : ( Task.Parallel.State2 String Int, Cmd Msg )
    doTask =
        attempt2
            TaskStateUpdated
            fetchString
            fetchInt
values.type: (Task.Parallel.Msg2 x a b -> msg) -> Task.Task x a -> Task.Task x b -> ( Task.Parallel.State2 a b, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: attempt3
values.type: (Task.Parallel.Msg3 x a b c -> msg) -> Task.Task x a -> Task.Task x b -> Task.Task x c -> ( Task.Parallel.State3 a b c, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: attempt4
values.type: (Task.Parallel.Msg4 x a b c d -> msg) -> Task.Task x a -> Task.Task x b -> Task.Task x c -> Task.Task x d -> ( Task.Parallel.State4 a b c d, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: attempt5
values.type: (Task.Parallel.Msg5 x a b c d e -> msg) -> Task.Task x a -> Task.Task x b -> Task.Task x c -> Task.Task x d -> Task.Task x e -> ( Task.Parallel.State5 a b c d e, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: attemptList
values.comment:
Attempt a list of tasks which will update when all the tasks have finished
or when one fails. Similar to a `Task.sequence` except in parallel.

    type Msg
        = DownloadUpdated (ListMsg Http.Error String)
        | DownloadFailed Http.Error
        | DownloadCompleted (List String)

    fetchNames : ( ListState String, nextCmd )
    fetchNames =
        attemptList DownloadUpdated [ fetchFirstName, fetchSecondName, fetchThirdName ]
values.type: (Task.Parallel.ListMsg x a -> msg) -> List.List (Task.Task x a) -> ( Task.Parallel.ListState a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: update2
values.comment:
Handle updates for two tasks by calling `update2` inside of your main update
function to keep this library's internal state updated. If they have either all
finished successfully or one has failed, the corresponding message you provided
will be sent to your main `update` function. Maintain a copy of the returned
state to pass in on each subsequent `update`. This step is required with
`attempt[n]` functions.

    type Msg
        = DownloadUpdated (Task.Parallel.Msg2 Http.Error Actor Film)
        | DownloadFailed Http.Error
        | DownloadCompleted Actor Film

    update : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    update msg model =
        case msg of
            DownloadUpdated taskMsg ->
                let
                    ( nextTaskState, nextCmd ) =
                        Task.Parallel.update2 model.taskState taskMsg DownloadCompleted DownloadFailed
                in
                ( { model | taskState = nextTaskState }, nextCmd )

            DownloadCompleted actor film ->
                ( { model | actor = actor, film = film, Cmd.none )

            DownloadFailed err ->
                ( { model | loadingError = Just err }, Cmd.none )
values.type: Task.Parallel.State2 a b -> Task.Parallel.Msg2 x a b -> (a -> b -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> ( Task.Parallel.State2 a b, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: update3
values.type: Task.Parallel.State3 a b c -> Task.Parallel.Msg3 x a b c -> (a -> b -> c -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> ( Task.Parallel.State3 a b c, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: update4
values.type: Task.Parallel.State4 a b c d -> Task.Parallel.Msg4 x a b c d -> (a -> b -> c -> d -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> ( Task.Parallel.State4 a b c d, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: update5
values.type: Task.Parallel.State5 a b c d e -> Task.Parallel.Msg5 x a b c d e -> (a -> b -> c -> d -> e -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> ( Task.Parallel.State5 a b c d e, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updateList
values.comment:
Call `updateList` inside of your main update function to check if the
tasks have failed or finished. Maintain a copy of the returned state to pass in
on each subsequent `updateList`. This step is required with
[`attemptList`](#attemptList).

    type Msg
        = DownloadUpdated (Task.Parallel.ListMsg Actor Http.Error)
        | DownloadFailed Http.Error
        | DownloadCompleted (List Actor)

    update : Msg -> Model -> ( Model, Cmd Msg )
    update msg model =
        case msg of
            DownloadUpdated taskMsg ->
                let
                    ( nextTaskState, nextCmd ) =
                        Task.Parallel.updateList model.taskState taskMsg DownloadCompleted DownloadFailed
                in
                ( { model | taskState = nextTaskState }, nextCmd )

            DownloadCompleted actors ->
                ( { model | actorList = actors, Cmd.none )

            DownloadFailed err ->
                ( { model | loadingError = Just err }, Cmd.none )
values.type: Task.Parallel.ListState a -> Task.Parallel.ListMsg x a -> (List.List a -> msg) -> (x -> msg) -> ( Task.Parallel.ListState a, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )
